Defines the ActionMenuLayer structure and functions for creating and managing action menus.

# Purpose
The code defines an interface for managing an `ActionMenuLayer` in a graphical user interface. It provides a set of functions to create, configure, and destroy an `ActionMenuLayer`, which is a specialized UI component that displays a menu with selectable items. The `ActionMenuLayer` structure includes components such as a `Layer`, a `MenuLayer`, and a callback function for handling item selection. It also manages layout and animation details through `ActionMenuLayoutCache` and `ActionMenuItemAnimation` structures.

The code includes functions to set the callback for item selection, configure the alignment of the menu, and manage the items displayed in the menu. It also provides functions to initialize and deinitialize the `ActionMenuLayer`, ensuring proper setup and teardown of resources. The inclusion of various headers suggests that this code is part of a larger application framework, likely related to UI and graphics management. The `ActionMenuLayer` is designed to be flexible, allowing for customization of menu items and their appearance, and is intended to be integrated into applications that require interactive menu functionality.
# Imports and Dependencies

- `action_menu_window.h`
- `click.h`
- `inverter_layer.h`
- `layer.h`
- `menu_layer.h`
- `scroll_layer.h`
- `applib/graphics/graphics.h`
- `applib/ui/animation.h`
- `applib/ui/window_private.h`
- `system/passert.h`

# Data Structures

---
### ActionMenuLayoutCache
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - `align`: Specifies the alignment of the action menu.
    - `font`: Defines the font used in the action menu.
    - `item_heights`: Points to an array of item heights in the action menu.
- **Description**: Stores layout-related information for an action menu, including alignment, font, and item heights, to facilitate rendering and layout calculations.


---
### ActionMenuItemAnimation
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``animation``: A pointer to an `Animation` structure.
    - ``top_offset_y``: An integer representing the top offset in the y-axis.
    - ``bottom_offset_y``: An integer representing the bottom offset in the y-axis.
    - ``current_offset_y``: An integer representing the current offset in the y-axis.
    - ``fade_top``: A `GBitmap` representing the top fade effect.
    - ``fade_bottom``: A `GBitmap` representing the bottom fade effect.
- **Description**: Defines the animation properties for an action menu item, including offsets for positioning and fade effects for visual transitions.


---
### ActionMenuLayer
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - `layer`: A `Layer` object that represents the base graphical layer.
    - `menu_layer`: A `MenuLayer` object that manages the menu interface.
    - `selected_index`: An integer that indicates the currently selected menu item index.
    - `separator_index`: An unsigned integer that specifies the index of the separator in the menu.
    - `cb`: A callback function of type `ActionMenuLayerCallback` for menu actions.
    - `items`: A pointer to an array of `ActionMenuItem` objects representing the menu items.
    - `num_items`: An integer that indicates the number of menu items.
    - `layout_cache`: An `ActionMenuLayoutCache` object used internally for layout management.
    - `item_animation`: An `ActionMenuItemAnimation` object used internally for item animations.
    - `short_items`: A pointer to an array of `ActionMenuItem` objects representing short menu items.
    - `num_short_items`: An integer that indicates the number of short menu items.
    - `context`: A pointer to user-defined data passed to the callback function.
- **Description**: Manages a menu interface with selectable items and supports custom callbacks for menu actions. It includes graphical layers, menu management, and internal structures for layout and animation. The structure allows for the configuration of menu items, separators, and short items, and it provides a context for callback functions.


# Function Declarations (Public API)

### action\_menu\_layer\_create<!-- {{#callable_declaration:action_menu_layer_create}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_layer.h#L72>)

Creates a new action menu layer with a specified frame.
- **Description**: Use this function to create a new `ActionMenuLayer` with a given frame. This function allocates memory for the `ActionMenuLayer` and initializes it with the specified frame dimensions. It is important to check the return value to ensure that the allocation was successful. If the allocation fails, the function returns `NULL`. After creation, you can configure the action menu layer further using other provided functions. Ensure to destroy the layer with `action_menu_layer_destroy` when it is no longer needed to free resources.
- **Inputs**:
    - `frame`: A `GRect` structure that defines the size and position of the action menu layer. The caller retains ownership of this structure, and it must be valid when passed to the function.
- **Output**: Returns a pointer to the newly created `ActionMenuLayer` if successful, or `NULL` if memory allocation fails.
- **See Also**: [`action_menu_layer_create`](<action_menu_layer.c.md#action_menu_layer_create>)  (Implementation)


---
### action\_menu\_layer\_set\_callback<!-- {{#callable_declaration:action_menu_layer_set_callback}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_layer.h#L74>)

Sets the callback function and context for an action menu layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to assign a callback function and a context pointer to an `ActionMenuLayer`. This function is typically called after creating the `ActionMenuLayer` and before displaying it, to ensure that the callback is invoked when an action menu item is selected. The callback function will receive the selected item and the context pointer as arguments. Ensure that the `ActionMenuLayer` pointer is valid and initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `aml`: A pointer to an `ActionMenuLayer` structure. Must not be null and must point to a valid, initialized `ActionMenuLayer`.
    - `cb`: A function pointer to the callback function that will be called when an action menu item is selected. Can be null if no callback is needed.
    - `context`: A pointer to user-defined data that will be passed to the callback function. Can be null if no context is needed.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`action_menu_layer_set_callback`](<action_menu_layer.c.md#action_menu_layer_set_callback>)  (Implementation)


---
### action\_menu\_layer\_set\_align<!-- {{#callable_declaration:action_menu_layer_set_align}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_layer.h#L78>)

Sets the alignment of the action menu layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to specify the alignment of an `ActionMenuLayer`. This function must be called with a valid `ActionMenuLayer` pointer. If the pointer is null, the function does nothing. This function is useful when you need to adjust the visual alignment of the menu items within the layer.
- **Inputs**:
    - `aml`: A pointer to an `ActionMenuLayer` structure. Must not be null. If null, the function returns immediately without making changes.
    - `align`: An `ActionMenuAlign` value that specifies the desired alignment for the action menu layer. This parameter determines how the menu items are aligned within the layer.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`action_menu_layer_set_align`](<action_menu_layer.c.md#action_menu_layer_set_align>)  (Implementation)


---
### action\_menu\_layer\_set\_items<!-- {{#callable_declaration:action_menu_layer_set_items}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_layer.h#L81>)

Sets the items for an action menu layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to define the items that an `ActionMenuLayer` will display. It sets the list of items, the number of items, and the index of a separator item if applicable. It also updates the default selected item in the menu. Call this function after creating an `ActionMenuLayer` and before displaying it to ensure the menu shows the correct items. Ensure that the `items` pointer is valid and that `num_items` accurately reflects the number of items in the array.
- **Inputs**:
    - `aml`: A pointer to an `ActionMenuLayer` structure. Must not be null. The function modifies this structure to set the menu items.
    - `items`: A pointer to an array of `ActionMenuItem` structures. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership of the array.
    - `num_items`: The number of items in the `items` array. Must be a non-negative integer.
    - `default_selected_item`: The index of the item that should be selected by default. Must be a valid index within the range of `num_items`.
    - `separator_index`: The index of the item that acts as a separator. Must be a valid index within the range of `num_items` or a special value if no separator is needed.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`action_menu_layer_set_items`](<action_menu_layer.c.md#action_menu_layer_set_items>)  (Implementation)

### action\_menu\_layer\_click\_config\_provider<!-- {{#callable_declaration:action_menu_layer_click_config_provider}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_layer.h#L87>)

Configures click event handlers for an action menu layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to set up the click event handlers for an `ActionMenuLayer`. It configures the UP and DOWN buttons to trigger a repeating scroll action and the SELECT button to trigger a selection action. This function must be called to ensure that the action menu layer responds to user input correctly. Ensure that the `ActionMenuLayer` is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `aml`: A pointer to an `ActionMenuLayer` structure. Must not be null. The function uses this parameter to set the context for click events.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`action_menu_layer_click_config_provider`](<action_menu_layer.c.md#action_menu_layer_click_config_provider>)  (Implementation)

[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_layer.h#L89>)

Releases resources associated with an action menu layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to free all resources associated with an `ActionMenuLayer` when it is no longer needed. This function must be called to prevent memory leaks. It is safe to call this function with a null pointer, in which case it does nothing. Ensure that the `ActionMenuLayer` is not used after calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `aml`: A pointer to the `ActionMenuLayer` to destroy. It can be null, in which case the function does nothing. The caller must ensure that the `ActionMenuLayer` is not used after this function is called.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`action_menu_layer_destroy`](<action_menu_layer.c.md#action_menu_layer_destroy>)  (Implementation)

[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/action_menu_layer.h#L91>)

Sets the short items for an action menu layer.
- **Description**: Use this function to define a subset of items, referred to as short items, for an `ActionMenuLayer`. This function is useful when you want to display a limited number of items in the menu. Ensure that the `ActionMenuLayer` is properly initialized before calling this function. The function updates the menu layer with the specified short items and sets the default selected item. It does not validate the input parameters, so ensure that the `items` pointer is valid and `num_items` is non-negative.
- **Inputs**:
    - `aml`: A pointer to an `ActionMenuLayer` structure. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `items`: A pointer to an array of `ActionMenuItem` structures representing the short items. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `num_items`: The number of items in the `items` array. Must be non-negative.
    - `default_selected_item`: The index of the item to be selected by default. Must be within the range of `num_items`.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`action_menu_layer_set_short_items`](<action_menu_layer.c.md#action_menu_layer_set_short_items>)  (Implementation)

Deinitializes an `ActionMenuLayer` instance.

This function must be called to prevent memory leaks and ensure that all associated resources are properly released. It is important to call this function only after the `ActionMenuLayer` has been initialized and used, and before the program terminates or the layer is discarded.
- **Inputs**:
    - `aml`: A pointer to an `ActionMenuLayer` instance. Must not be null. The function assumes that the `ActionMenuLayer` has been previously initialized. Passing a null pointer or an uninitialized `ActionMenuLayer` results in undefined behavior.
